content.subtitle: Place
rows: There are amazing waterfalls **in** the rainforest.
Your passport is **in** the top drawer.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: in a minute / an hour / a year

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: With movement verbs (go, come, walk, run, move, fly), we often use **to** for destination: *go to Japan*.

content.subtitle: Place
rows: She left her glasses **on** the coffee table.
There's a map of the world **on** the wall.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **General vs. Specific**: We say *in the morning/afternoon/evening*, but we use **on** when a specific day is mentioned (**on Monday morning** / **on Wednesday evening** / etc.).
✓ We're flying to Washington in the morning.
✓ We're flying to Washington on Tuesday morning.
**No Preposition**: Do not use a preposition with *tomorrow*, *yesterday*, *tomorrow morning*, *yesterday evening*, etc.
✓ We're flying to Washington tomorrow afternoon.

content.subtitle: Time
rows: The film starts **at** quarter past eight.
Are you visiting your family **at** Easter?

content.subtitle: Place
rows: It gets very cold **at** the top of the mountain.
They used to live **at** 7 Park Avenue.
She must be **at** the gym — she said she was going this afternoon.
He couldn't come to the phone. He was **at** a meeting.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: It is important to compare how we use **in** and **at** for places:
**In**: Used for larger areas that are all around us when we are there. Example: *We spent our holiday in the mountains.*
**At**: Used for smaller places and specific points on a journey. Example: *Can you meet me at the bus stop?*

sections.title: A: Tick if correct; correct if wrong
sections.instructions: If the preposition in bold is correct, write ✓. If it is wrong, write the correct preposition.

items.sentence: We first visited China on 2006.
My birthday is at the second of July.
Let's meet on five o'clock, shall we?
School starts again in September.
There's a party at Emily's at Saturday.
What do you want to do on the morning?
Let's go and see Grandma on Easter.
Where do you usually go in Christmas Day?

sections.title: B: Complete using on, in or at
sections.instructions: Write on, in or at in each gap.

items.sentence: There are lots of people ...... the restaurant.
The people who live ...... number 44 are away on holiday.
You should go to the Louvre when you're ...... Paris.
Gorillas live ...... forests in Africa and eat fruit.
What does that sign ...... the wall say?
What did Ethan say ...... his letter?
Have you heard of the strange statues ...... Easter Island?
Do you really want to spend the whole day ...... the beach?

sections.title: C: Look at the pictures and complete
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with in, on or at.

items.sentence: This photo was taken ...... winter.
We're ...... a concert.
She's ...... the sea.
It's ...... page 62.
It's ...... the middle.
He's ...... an island.
It's ...... the mountain.
They're ...... a wedding.

sections.title: D: Complete using words in the box
sections.instructions: Use the words in the box to complete the sentences.

items.sentence: My aunt and uncle have decided to move ...... New Zealand.
Do you want to go ...... the theatre tomorrow?
We stayed ...... a great hotel in Dubai.
Wait ...... the end of the street and I'll come and meet you.
You can come ...... my house for dinner, if you like.
Connor was walking ...... the corner shop when he realised he'd lost his wallet.
We drove all night and finally arrived ...... Lisbon at eight o'clock.
Did you leave your book ...... the teacher's desk, so she can see it?
Look at those sheep ...... that field over there.
It takes about six hours to fly ...... Asia from here.

sections.title: E: Circle the correct word
sections.instructions: Choose the correct preposition in each sentence.

items.sentence: I'm meeting Andy **at / on** the cinema in an hour.
Have you seen the new building **at / in** front of the school?
My new job starts **in / on** the first day of August.
We're going to Martin's to see their new baby **in / on** Wednesday evening.
See if there are any tomatoes **at / in** the fridge, will you?
We'll all have computers connected to our brains **at / in** the future.
I don't feel like playing chess **at / on** the moment.
I think there's someone **at / in** the door. I'll go and check.

sections.title: F: Complete the text (Jetlag)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: When you travel ...... the other side of the world, jetlag is a real problem.
You find yourself awake ...... the middle of the night.
...... the morning, just when everyone around you is getting up.
Jetlag happens when you go ...... a country where the time is very different.
For example, you might leave London ...... midday.
And fly ...... Los Angeles.
When you arrive ...... Los Angeles airport, your body thinks you're there.
Your body thinks you're there ...... 11 pm.
So you actually get there ...... 3 pm local time.
So, ...... midnight Los Angeles time, your body thinks it's morning.
It still thinks it's ...... London.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one wrong preposition. Write the correct one.

items.sentence: We usually go skiing on winter.
Let's meet in 7:30 outside the station.
My parents got married at 12th June.
She left her phone at the table in the kitchen.
They're waiting in the door for you.
We arrived to Madrid very late.
I love walking at the beach in the evening.
He'll be back on a few minutes.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
The lesson starts at nine o'clock. **ON**
The lesson doesn't start ...... the afternoon.

My exam is on Monday morning. **IN**
My exam isn't ...... the evening.

She is currently at a meeting. **MOMENT**
She is at a meeting ...... .

We landed in Rome at midnight. **AT**
We landed in Rome ...... .

Let's meet outside the cinema entrance. **DOOR**
Let's meet ...... the cinema.

The keys are in the centre of the table. **MIDDLE**
The keys are ...... the table.
Our train leaves ...... half past six.
I was born ...... 2010.
We're having lunch ...... Sunday.
There's a photo ...... the wall.
My sister lives ...... Canada.
We'll see you ...... Christmas.
He put the bag ...... the floor.
They drove ...... the airport very early.

sections.title: J: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one preposition.

items.sentence: We arrived ...... the station at 6:45.
Do you like reading ...... bed?
I always feel sleepy ...... the afternoon.
Can you come to my house ...... Friday evening?
There's a small village ...... the coast.
My grandparents live ...... 16 Green Street.
Let's go ...... the mountains next weekend.
I left your notebook ...... my bag.
They're waiting ...... the bus stop.
I'll call you ...... a few minutes.
